item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpick, but when you say that the emoticons are typed using QWERTY do you mean they're literally aware that this thing is somehow using a keyboard or that the characters used map to the letters available on such a set? If it's the latter I feel like the foundation would be more likely to refer to it as writing in the ASCII character set or something like that than a specific keyboard layout.

That aside this is amazing and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit, I was highly skeptical of this piece. It seemed to be just a "strange thing that talks poorly", and it is. But I found it fun enough to read despite myself, and the very last test put a smile on my face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm back cause I keep laughing at this. Imagine the field agents trying to retrieve this thing and walking into the invisible side of the ship and running their hands along the entire fucking thing and slowly realizing with horror that it's a goddamn ship. Like that scene in Star Trek IV. This is so funny, great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. The title is fucking amazing too. I was laughing just waiting for the article to load.

I think the extended test log is a bit unnecessary? It gets a little dado-y at that point. But that's certainly not enough to ruin the charm of a great article. Big +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sieve gave me the SCP idea, so I kind of owed it to the little guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that is disgusting, but I mean … you do what you gotta do ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took a picture of a sieve I pee in when I get kidney stones for an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope the upvote button is okay. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Coffe. drinky drinky."

Slammed the upvote button.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Fixed the stuff you mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
in case a of failure to bring caffeine

Did you mean an of?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I feel that this one is an interesting idea held back from being its best by some issues with clunky word flow, run-on sentences, and a dash of non-clinical tone.

Here are a few examples:

"The substance produced by SCP-4944 does not have any hazardous effects, but is extremely viscid, and has high levels of glucose and gelatin, causing the fluid to be highly sweet when tasted."
Break this up into smaller sentences.

"A test was conducted on SCP-4944 to answer several questions."
Non-clinical tone. Just call it an interview, or label it as "sentience test (id#)

"After several more questions, it is confirmed "
Clinical tone, present tense abuse. try something like "has been determined"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ll always upvote a fellow coffee lover

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, I did take my coffee machine into the freezing cold back yard to take that picture, and no, I am not proud of it.

I say you should be proud. I took a picture of a roach for an SCP. One of my co-authors purposely ordered a gross-looking pizza for an SCP.

These are the things we do for our craft

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually don't drink coffee, but my coworker can't survive without the stuff. I need to get him a shirt with "Don't care, need bean." on it.

So, I liked this a lot in the ideas stage, and I'm glad to see it on the mainsite! The table is hilarious and just the right amount of punchy (I've seen lots of drafts with hecking looooonng table content, so this is a nice change of pace for me). I almost feel like you don't necessarily need the experiment log since the rest entertained me just fine, but I love the dialogue.

ETA: general note, might want to swap "sentience" with "sapience" and make the Footnotes left-justified? It seems a little weird to have them centered.

+1 for quirky boat that just needs the coffee!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for reading ! This idea actually came from my own coffee machine (seen in the actual entry) because the loud ass fog-horn noises it makes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh, finally. An SCP I can relate with.
On an actual note, I was pleasantly caught off guard with this wacky entry! Definite +1 here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, this cliche is becoming very popular. The goal of the whole talking shtick was to really grab that weird and absurd plot I was going for. I respect your choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well-written, but the "anomalous object talks like an 'ironic' twitter user" shtick is really starting to wear thin, in my opinion.

Novoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't care, need bean.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess you could say I was really quite thirsty,
And that's a statement I can say real firmly,
For the longer I looked at it my thirst starts to grow,
All I wanted was a damn cup o' joe.

I tried to get through but something resisted,
Something invisible was there and existed.
All I needed was some freakin' caffeine,
That god damn coffee machine.

So yeah, this exists now.
Thanks to UraniumEmpire, JustPoko, and Zyn for the critiques, I really appreciate it.

First Image is by me, second image is here, and edited by me.

m͎o͎r͎e͎ ͎b͎y͎ ͎m͎e͎

Yes, I did take my coffee machine into the freezing cold back yard to take that picture, and no, I am not proud of it. Okay… maybe a little proud.
